page: Amazon Web Services c6id.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The configuration provides 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Memory resources consist of 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 950 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 6 Gbps.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX-512 (specifically avx512f and avx512_vnni), and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. In performance testing, the instance achieves a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1892.39 and a multi-core score of 15460.27. Geekbench testing yields a single-core score of 1643.0 and a multi-core score of 9833.0, with software compilation (clang) measured at 15294.0 and ray tracing at 17640.0.

Memory bandwidth tests show a cached read-only small block speed of 828.67 GB/sec, dropping to 99.02 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, and a write speed of 56.68 GB/sec. The instance demonstrates strong text generation speeds for small language models (135M) at 258.84 tokens/sec, while prompt processing for the same model reaches 1143.43 tokens/sec. For database operations, the Passmark database score is 7860.76, and Redis SET operations reach 15.68 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is recorded at 21.31 GB/sec.

The c6id.4xlarge offers a balanced compute-to-storage profile by bundling 950 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage directly with dedicated compute resources. This design provides qualitative cost advantages for workloads requiring low-latency local scratch space, eliminating the need for separate network-attached storage. However, the 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU represents a tradeoff, making this instance less suitable for memory-intensive applications that exceed the 32.0 GB system limit. Appropriate workloads include database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance across most categories, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 9833.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 21278.81. It demonstrates strong text generation performance for small language models at 258.84 tokens/second. The 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU represents a tradeoff for memory-heavy tasks, but the integrated NVMe storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive operations. This instance is suited for database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and distributed compute workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 950 GB NVMe SSD, suited for database operations and software compilation.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.4xlarge features 16 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 950 GB NVMe SSD storage for compute-intensive and database workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR4 memory, and integrated high-speed NVMe local SSD storage.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor at 3.5 GHz.
32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM providing 2.0 GB per core.
950 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage.
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving.
